title: Cloud Atlas activity in the second half of 2025 and early 2026: new tools and a new payload
summary: Cloud Atlas attacks the public sector and diplomatic structures of Russia and Belarus, using ReverseSocks, SSH, and Tor for persistence in infected systems and its new tool, PowerCloud.

title: How an image could compromise your Mac: understanding an ExifTool vulnerability (CVE-2026-3102)
summary: We explain how a flaw in ExifTool allows attackers to compromise macOS systems via a malicious image (CVE-2026-3102).

title: IT threat evolution in Q1 2026. Mobile statistics
summary: This report contains mobile threat statistics for Q1 2026, along with noteworthy discoveries and quarterly trends: new versions of SparkCat and Triada.

title: IT threat evolution in Q1 2026. Non-mobile statistics
summary: The report presents key trends and statistics on malware that targeted personal computers running Windows and macOS, as well as Internet of Things (IoT) devices, during Q1 2026.

title: Kimsuky targets organizations with PebbleDash-based tools
summary: Kaspersky researchers analyze a range of new PebbleDash-based tools used in recent Kimsuky campaigns and reveal their connection to the AppleSeed malware cluster.

title: State of ransomware in 2026
summary: Kaspersky researchers are sharing insights into the main ransomware trends for 2026: EDR killers on the rise, switching from data encryption to data leaks, and more.

title: CVE-2025-68670: discovering an RCE vulnerability in xrdp
summary: During a security assessment of Kaspersky USB Redirector, we discovered CVE-2025-68670: a pre-auth RCE in the xrdp server component. Project maintainers promptly patched the vulnerability.

title: Exploits and vulnerabilities in Q1 2026
summary: This report provides statistical data on published vulnerabilities and exploits we researched during Q1 2026. It also includes summary data on the use of C2 frameworks in APT attacks.

title: OceanLotus suspected of using PyPI to deliver ZiChatBot malware
summary: Kaspersky researchers uncovered malicious wheel packages in PyPI that targeted both Windows and Linux and contained a dropper delivering malware dubbed ZiChatBot. We attribute this activity to OceanLotus APT.

title: Websites with an undefined trust level: avoiding the trap
summary: We explain what suspicious websites are and how to distinguish a safe site from a fraudulent one. A new category in Kaspersky solutions: we're sharing global statistics on untrusted site detection.
